---
title: Pixi configuration
---

Pixi provides a reproducible command environment for Quarto projects. The
same declared tasks can be run by contributors locally and by automated
publishing on GitHub.

## Dependencies and tasks

A minimal project may declare Quarto and tasks in `pixi.toml`:

```toml
[dependencies]
quarto = ">=1.8,<2"
python = "3.12.*"

[tasks.preview]
cmd = "quarto preview"
cwd = "course_notes"

[tasks.render]
cmd = "quarto render"
cwd = "course_notes"
```

Some repositories keep equivalent configuration under `[tool.pixi]` in
`pyproject.toml`.

## Task arguments and profiles

Projects that publish multiple views can accept task arguments:

```toml
[tasks.preview]
args = [{ "arg" = "mode", default = "student",
          choices = ["student", "instructor"] }]
cmd = "quarto preview --profile {{mode}}"
cwd = "course"
```

This is why the command a contributor runs must come from that repository's
documentation or task manifest: profile labels and defaults differ.

## Supporting tasks

Pixi tasks may also:

- Clean output before a complete render.
- Fetch fonts or other build prerequisites.
- Run figure, download, or domain-specific script checks.
- Authenticate with GitHub.
- Collect or rename generated notebooks for distribution.

These helpers reflect the needs of an individual project and should not be
assumed to exist elsewhere.

## Lock files and CI

`pixi.lock` records resolved package versions for repeatable environments.
GitHub Actions workflows commonly install Pixi and run `pixi run render` or
`pixi run render-all`, which keeps publishing aligned with the local
contributor workflow.
